title: Level 740
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 740: "Movieception Theatre"}}{{Headerbox|file=Level 194 image 3.jpg|speed=20|header=Welcome to Movieception Theatre!}}{{Class Variable}}
== Description ==
'''Level 740''', also known as “'''Movieception Theatre'''”, is an expansive cinema complex made out of of numerous auditoriums that posses unique properties. These auditoriums are interconnected by corridors. This level has no staircases present, making it one massive floor. Due to the constant illumination from the LEDs, there are no dark areas anywhere in the hallways. Notably, there are no light switches, and the source of electricity powering the lighting remains unknown.

{{Blurimage|file=Level 194 photo 1.jpg|caption=A photo of one of the many hallways found in this level.}}

== The Hallways ==
The Hallways are the most common area of the level, and typically serve as the main location for wanderers upon entry. This section is composed of tons of corridors, resembling the interior hallways of an average cinema dating from 2020. The Hallways are entirely devoid of litter, as any object dropped to the ground will noclip through the floor. Each hallway extends over 300 meters in length,  and are approximately 2 meters in width. The walls are made of concrete and follows a dual-color scheme.

=== White Walls ===
These walls appear blue due to the ambient blue LED lighting that spans the entire level. Fire extinguishers, trapped in glass, are found along these walls.

=== Black Walls ===
These walls have large doors that lead to other halls; however, these doors are locked, forcing wanderers to travel the hallways end to find an exit.

The flooring alternates between gray marble and worn, arcade style carpeting. The carpeted sections are often riddled with mold, moisture, and occasional holes, revealing the dirt ground beneath. The ceiling is constructed from polished blue marble and is embedded with the aforementioned LED lighting.

== The Food Zones ==
The Food Zone is the least frequented area within the theatre complex. It spans approximately 100 square meters and follows a similar style to the surrounding hallways. However, notable differences include the floor, which always maintains a clean, arcade-like style. Unlike other sections, this flooring is dry, free of holes, and hot enough to cause burns if one is barefoot, making shoes strongly advised to be put on before entering this area. The walls remain black and white, consistent with the rest of the theatre, while the ceiling is outfitted with LED lights. These lights often flicker or are broken, although some may occasionally function correctly. Throughout The Food Zone, white food court styled tables are placed at regular intervals. Notably, there are no chairs accompanying them.

The back of the Food Zone has a door labeled “maintenance”. Behind it lies a large storage like room, with unusually arranged soda dispensers. This room covers roughly 20 square meters and features walls, floors, and ceilings made entirely of white concrete. It is also the only area that is brightly illuminated, with white light bulbs covering the entire ceiling, and these lights cannot be turned off. The floor is scattered with red plastic cups, some knocked over, others upright, and some are reminiscent of standard fast-food cups, which can be used to dispense soda.  However, some sodas are hazardous.{{Blurimage|file=194 image 2.jpg|caption=An image of one of the many soda dispensers.}}

=== Soda Dispensers ===
There are five distinct types of soda, each with unique effects.
{| class="fandom-table"
|+
!'''Color'''
!'''Side Effects'''
!'''Inside'''
|-
!Purple
!Induces a temporary shrinking effect, reducing the individual to ant size for approximately two hours.
!Constantly shrinking, though has the same volume.

{{Blurimage|file=Level 194 image 3.jpg|caption=An image of one of the many auditoriums from this level. This one has regular seats only.}}

== The Auditoriums ==
The Auditoriums serve as the primary sections of this level, notably matching the size of an average frontroom cinema. Architecturally, they resemble the hallways of the level, with a few distinct differences, with the walls being painted black, the flooring always covered in a patterned arcade style carpet, and the lighting consists of blue and red LED lights arranged in circular patterns along the walls, with blue lights shining more brightly than red lights.

Each auditorium is fully equipped with rows of seating that possess a unique property. Films are only projected when a person sits on a seat. There are six distinct types of seats, and the specific seat occupied determines which film will be displayed. The most common of these is the Regular Seat, which is frequent enough that some auditoriums contain only this type.

=== Movies ===
Once a movie begins, the individual will view a film corresponding to the type of seat they sat on. Each film features a Faceling as the main character, with the storyline appearing to be randomized for every viewer. However, a consistent element across all experiences, which is the setting in that the movie unfolds.

Viewers can physically approach the screen, which is made out of a strange, liquid-like substance with unknown properties. Upon entering the screen, the individual will immediately lose consciousness and awaken within the movie itself, now assuming the role of the main character in place of the Faceling. As of now, M.E.G. has documented 10 movies.{{Blurimage|file=Movie 1.jpg|caption=An image of Movie 1, capturing one of the enormous rides.}}

==== Movie 1 ====
Movie 1, or "Megalophobic Carnival", is set in an expansive, nighttime carnival surrounded by an infinite forest, which presents an unsettling atmosphere. The forest features hostile entities, particularly Deathmoths, posing a constant threat to explorers. Within the carnival are oversized versions of familiar attractions.

M.E.G. agents have attempted to locate multiple exits from the area, but only two have proven functional. Either entering a hidden maintenance door leading to Movie 3, or touching a mysterious spike transports a wanderer to Movie 9.{{Blurimage|file=Movie 2.png|caption=An image of one of the skinny houses, found in Movie 2.}}

==== Movie 2 ====
Movie 2, or "Skinny Neighborhood", resembles as a cold suburban neighborhood. The area is surrounded by a seemingly endless rocky field, devoid of any vegetation. Each house in the neighborhood is noticeably narrow, with one being 10 centimeters wide. Upon entering, one finds themselves in a massive, empty room, with walls and flooring resembling the wallpaper from [[Level 0]]. The entire neighborhood is devoid of entities. The only known exit is through a tunnel at the end of the main road, leading to Movie 6.{{Blurimage|file=Movie 3.jpg|caption=An image of the hallways of Movie 3.}}

==== Movie 3 ====
"Doors, Doors, Doors", or Movie 3, is set in a circular corridor, approximately 500 meters long. This movie features an unusual number of locked doors lining both walls. The walls, floor, and ceiling are made of blue concrete, and the hallway is infested with Clumps. Due to their high numbers, remaining here for extended periods is strongly not advised. The only way to escape is by locating a single unlocked door, which leads to Movie 5.

==== Movie 4 ====
Happiness Paradise is the safest of the documented films. This movie is set in an infinite medieval castle submerged in Almond Water. While the walls extend upward endlessly, there is no access to the outside world. Floating throughout the flooded interior are pieces of furniture. Graffiti arrows, painted by an unidentified entity, guide explorers through the castle.{{Blurimage|file=Movie 5.jpg|caption=An image of the forest found in movie 5.}}

==== Movie 5 ====
Named "Abyssal Forest", this movie is set  in a thick, fog-covered forest, this movie combines harsh snow with a hot climate, and exposure can result in sunburn despite the cold environment. The forest spans roughly 400 meters in every direction, ending in a surrounding void.

Falling into the void, or the holes scattered in the Movie, will transport a wanderer to Movie 10.{{Blurimage|file=Movie 6.jpg|caption=An image of the highway of movie 6, there are many STOP signs around it.}}

==== Movie 6 ====
Titled "Please, Stop!", this movie takes place on a looping highway, which appears to stretch infinitely, but eventually curves back to its starting point. Cold grass borders the highway, and contact with it may cause severe frostbite. Though devoid of entities, the highway is lined with unusually warm STOP signs that can cause burns upon touch, despite showing no signs of melting.

There is only one exit, and that is by finding a playground at the edge of the road and touching it, which leads the individual to Movie 7.{{Blurimage|file=Movie 7 .jpg|caption=An image of the playground in movie 7.}}

==== Movie 7 ====
The film, named "Mr. Darkwoods Playground", is set in a desolate playground, surrounded by jagged rocks embedded with shattered glass. A single streetlamp stands nearby, serving as the area’s only source of light. Childlike Facelings inhabit the playground, peacefully playing and showing no interest in other wanderers. 
{{Arcade Theme}}

To exit this level, locate an incongruous door situated near the playground, which leads to Movie 8.

==== Movie 8 ====
This movie, named "Retail Depression", is set in an expansive, dimly lit retail store. The farther one ventures from the illuminated area, the more Smilers begin to appear. The store lacks walls, and instead features concrete support poles holding up the ceiling.

The floor is coated in a thick, toxic ink that should not be consumed. Shelves are largely empty, though one shelf glows brightly, serving as the sole exit, leading to Movie 5.{{Blurimage|file=Movie 9.jpg|caption=An image of The Great Spike found in Movie 9.}}

==== Movie 9 ====
Taking place in a vast, snow-covered forest, "The Great Spike" is silent, except for the sound of continuous thunder, despite no visible lightning. At the center lies a massive pit, over one kilometer in diameter. And in the middle of this abyss stands a towering concrete spike, unreachable from the forest due to something separating them.

Falling into the abyss will transport one either to Movie 1, or Movie 4.

==== Movie 10 ====
"The Dark Abyss" is the final recorded film takes place in a pitch-black, enclosed box measuring 10 meters high, 5 meters wide, and 5 meters long. The structure is made entirely of cardboard, and the environment is completely silent. Within this darkness lurks a Deathmoth, posing a serious threat due to the lack of light.

== Entities ==
Smilers, Deathmoths,  Clumps, and Facelings can be found in various auditoriums and movies.

== Entrances and Exits ==

=== Entrances ===

* Finding a theatre called “Movieception Theatre” in [[Level 11]] will bring one here.

=== Exits ===

* Despite the arrows leading to the castle in Movie 4, it can also lead the individual to stumble upon a door to [[Level 4]].

* By breaking out of the cardboard box in Movie 10, the individual will fall down to the abyss to [[Level 741]].

* Noclipping through any doors, rarely sends one to [[Level 960]].

* Noclipping through one of the wallpapers from Movie 2 will send a wanderer to [[Level 0]].
